title: tragacantha litorea
intro: Tragacantha litorea, also known as the milk thistle, is a plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae) and is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used for centuries due to its medicinal properties, particularly its seeds, which contain silymarin, a flavonoid complex with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Tragacantha litorea has been traditionally used to support liver health, reduce inflammation, and improve overall well-being, and its health benefits include protecting against liver damage, supporting detoxification processes, and promoting antioxidant defenses. Herbal preparations of Tragacantha litorea include capsules, tablets, and teas, often standardized to contain a specific percentage of silymarin, which is used to treat liver diseases, including hepatitis and cirrhosis.
